content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN and a (000-indexed) binary string AAA having length N+1N+1N+1.

Find any permutation PPP of 0,1,2,...,NŌłÆ1{0,1,2,...,N-1}0,1,2,...,NŌłÆ1 (or determine that it does not exist) that satisfies the following conditions for all iii (0ŌēżiŌēżN0 \le i \le N0ŌēżiŌēżN):


if Ai=0A_i = 0AiŌĆŗ=0: No contiguous segment of PPP has mex\texttt{mex}mex equal to iii
if Ai=1A_i = 1AiŌĆŗ=1: There exists at least one contiguous segment of PPP that has mex\texttt{mex}mex equal to iii
If multiple permutations exist that satisfy the given conditions, print any.

Note: mex\texttt{mex}mex of a segment is the smallest non-negative number that does not occur in that segment.


Input Format

The first line contains the number of test cases TTT. Description of the test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains the binary string AAA of length N+1N + 1N+1.


Output Format
For each test case print :



Yes\texttt{Yes}Yes if there exists a permutation PPP that satisfies the conditions described in the statement, followed by the permutation PPP in the next line (If multiple permutations exist that satisfy the given conditions, print any).
No\texttt{No}No otherwise.

You may print each character of Yes\texttt{Yes}Yes and No\texttt{No}No in uppercase or lowercase (for example, yes\texttt{yes}yes, yEs\texttt{yEs}yEs, YES\texttt{YES}YES will be considered identical).
constraints: It is guaranteed that the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3Ōŗģ1053 \cdot 10^53Ōŗģ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case-1: One of the possible permutations satisfying the given conditions is [0,10, 10,1] because:

mex([1])=0\texttt{mex}([1]) = 0mex([1])=0. Therefore the condition is satisfied for i=0i = 0i=0.
mex([0])=1\texttt{mex}([0]) = 1mex([0])=1. Therefore the condition is satisfied for i=1i = 1i=1.
mex([0,1])=2\texttt{mex}([0, 1]) = 2mex([0,1])=2. Therefore the condition is satisfied for i=2i = 2i=2.

Test case-2: It can be proven that no permutation exists that satisfies the given conditions.
Test case-3: One of the possible permutations satisfying the given conditions is [0,2,1,4,30, 2, 1, 4, 30,2,1,4,3] because:

mex([2])=0\texttt{mex}([2]) = 0mex([2])=0. Therefore the condition is satisfied for i=0i = 0i=0.
mex([0,2])=1\texttt{mex}([0, 2]) = 1mex([0,2])=1. Therefore the condition is satisfied for i=1i = 1i=1.
There does not exist any segment with mex=2\texttt{mex} = 2mex=2. Therefore the condition is satisfied for i=2i = 2i=2.
mex([0,2,1])=3\texttt{mex}([0, 2, 1]) = 3mex([0,2,1])=3. Therefore the condition is satisfied for i=3i = 3i=3.
There does not exist any segment with mex=4\texttt{mex} = 4mex=4. Therefore the condition is satisfied for i=4i = 4i=4.
mex([0,2,1,4,3])=5\texttt{mex}([0, 2, 1, 4, 3]) = 5mex([0,2,1,4,3])=5. Therefore the condition is satisfied for i=5i = 5i=5.
